title: The man who fed the cats at dawn
summary: A quiet portrait of one daily kindness on a busy Cairo street.
topic: The man who fed the cats at dawn
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a person you saw doing a small daily kindness. Describe what they did and how it changed the way you saw the place. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On my third week in…, I started to wake up early because of the heat…
Every morning around five, an old man with a plastic bag…
He never looked up, but I could see…
sampleText: On my third week in Cairo, I started to wake up before five because of the heat. From my small balcony I could see a narrow alley between two old buildings. Every morning at exactly the same time, an old man in a grey shirt walked into the alley with a heavy plastic bag. He had a small white beard and walked with a slight limp. At first I thought he was collecting rubbish. After a few mornings I understood. He was feeding the stray cats. He put down two metal bowls, filled one with water from a bottle, and scattered small pieces of chicken into the other. About six or seven cats came down from the rooftops and ate without fighting. The man never spoke, never took a photo, never waited for anyone to see him. He just sat on a low step and watched them until the bowls were empty. After ten minutes he stood up, picked up the bowls, and walked away. I asked the woman who sold bread on the corner who he was. She said he had done the same thing every morning for almost twenty years. After that, Cairo did not feel as loud or as strange. I knew at least one person who was waking up to be kind.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a narrow alley between two old buildings
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một con ngõ hẹp giữa hai tòa nhà cũ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I thought he was collecting rubbish
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi tưởng ông đang nhặt rác

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He was feeding the stray cats
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông đang cho lũ mèo hoang ăn
rải những miếng thịt gà nhỏ
ăn mà không tranh giành nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: never waited for anyone to see him
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không bao giờ đợi ai nhìn thấy ông cả
cho đến khi những cái bát hết sạch

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he had done the same thing every morning for almost twenty years
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông đã làm cùng việc đó mỗi sáng gần hai mươi năm rồi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Cairo did not feel as loud or as strange
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cairo không còn cảm giác ồn ào hay xa lạ nữa

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous to correct an early assumption
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Past continuous tả việc đang diễn ra mà người kể chỉ hiểu ra sau: 'At first I thought he was collecting rubbish. After a few mornings I understood. He was feeding the stray cats.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Past continuous describes an ongoing situation the narrator only later understands: 'At first I thought he was collecting rubbish. After a few mornings I understood. He was feeding the stray cats.'

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'As + adjective + as' for comparison
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'As + tính từ + as' diễn tả mức độ ngang bằng (hoặc phủ định 'not as … as' để diễn tả không bằng): 'Cairo did not feel as loud or as strange.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'As + adjective + as' shows equal degree (or 'not as … as' for less than): 'Cairo did not feel as loud or as strange.'

sampleTranslationVi: Vào tuần thứ ba ở Cairo, tôi bắt đầu thức dậy trước năm giờ vì nóng. Từ ban công nhỏ tôi có thể thấy một con ngõ hẹp giữa hai tòa nhà cũ. Mỗi sáng đúng cùng một giờ, một ông cụ mặc áo xám đi vào con ngõ với một túi nilon nặng. Ông có một bộ râu trắng nhỏ và đi với hơi khập khiễng. Lúc đầu tôi tưởng ông đang nhặt rác. Sau vài buổi sáng tôi mới hiểu. Ông đang cho lũ mèo hoang ăn. Ông đặt xuống hai cái bát kim loại, đổ một cái đầy nước từ chai, và rải những miếng thịt gà nhỏ vào cái kia. Khoảng sáu hay bảy con mèo từ trên mái nhà đi xuống và ăn mà không tranh giành nhau. Ông không bao giờ nói gì, không bao giờ chụp ảnh, không bao giờ đợi ai nhìn thấy ông cả. Ông chỉ ngồi trên một bậc thềm thấp và ngắm chúng cho đến khi những cái bát hết sạch. Sau mười phút ông đứng dậy, cầm những cái bát lên, và đi mất. Tôi hỏi người phụ nữ bán bánh mì ở góc phố ông là ai. Bà nói ông đã làm cùng việc đó mỗi sáng gần hai mươi năm rồi. Sau đó, Cairo không còn cảm giác ồn ào hay xa lạ nữa. Tôi đã biết ít nhất một người đang thức dậy để làm điều tử tế.

options: He was feeding the stray cats.
He is feeding the stray cats.
He was feed the stray cats.
He has fed the stray cats.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'as + adjective + as' sentence.
options: Cairo did not feel as loud or as strange.
Cairo did not feel as louder or as stranger.
Cairo did not feel as loud nor strange as.
Cairo did not felt as loud or as strange.
a cat that has no owner and lives outside
a cat that belongs to a rich family
a cat that lives in a pet shop
a cat that is very small

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence with 'because of + noun'.
options: I started to wake up before five because of the heat.
I started to wake up before five because of it was hot.
I started to wake up before five because of hot.
I started to wake up before five because of the heat was.
He had done the same thing every morning for almost twenty years.
He has done the same thing every morning for almost twenty years.
He did the same thing every morning since almost twenty years.
He was doing the same thing every morning during almost twenty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect reports a duration leading up to the past point of the story.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural phrase: 'walked with a slight ___.'
options: limp
loud
lost
lift
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Walk with a slight limp' = walk slightly unevenly due to leg pain.

options: to throw small pieces over a wide area
to put neatly in one pile
to wash carefully
to lock in a box
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Scatter' = spread small pieces over an area.

options: The man never spoke, never took a photo.
The man not never spoke, not never took a photo.
The man speak never, take never a photo.
Never the man spoke, never the man took a photo.
He watched them until the bowls were empty.
He watched them while the bowls were empty.
He watched them because the bowls were empty.
He watched them since the bowls were empty.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Until' marks the end point of an action.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'between two old buildings'.
options: between
among
from
across
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Between' for two specific items; 'among' for three or more.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing sentence.
options: I knew at least one person who was waking up to be kind.
I know at least one person who waking up to be kind.
I knew at least one person who wakes up to was kind.
I knew at least one person who has waking up to be kind.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'knew' + relative clause with past continuous 'was waking up' fits the past frame.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do small kind actions, done without anyone watching, change a city in a way we cannot see? How?
